segments.title: Methods & Patterns
segments.description: This quadrant covers methods and patterns related to software development, including architecture, security, testing, organizational methodologies, and more.
segments.label: Quadrant 2

segments.title: Tools
segments.description: This quadrant features a variety of tools, from small utilities to large-scale software projects.
segments.label: Quadrant 4

segments.title: Platforms & Operations
segments.description: This quadrant clusters technologies focused on the operation of software, related platforms, tools, infrastructure, and services.
segments.label: Quadrant 3

segments.title: Languages & Frameworks
segments.description: This quadrant includes programming languages (e.g., Java and Golang) and frameworks (e.g., React and Spring Boot) that are at the core of custom software development.
segments.label: Quadrant 1

rings.title: Adopt
rings.description: We can clearly recommend this technology. We have used it for longer period of time in many teams and it has proven to be stable and useful.

rings.title: Trial
rings.description: We have used it with success and recommend to have a closer look at the technology in this ring. The goal of items here is to look at them more closely, with the goal to bring them to the adopt level.

rings.title: Assess
rings.description: We have tried it out and we find it promising. We recommend having a look at these items when you face a specific need for the technology in your project.

rings.title: Hold
rings.description: This category is a bit special. Unlike the others, we recommend to stop doing or using something. That does not mean that they are bad and it often might be ok to use them in existing projects. But we move things here if we think we shouldn't do them anymore - because we see better options or alternatives now.

labels.title: Technology Radar
labels.footer: Gunvor Group is one of the world's largest independent commodities trading houses by turnover. Our technology radar provides an overview of the technologies we use and recommend across our engineering teams.
labels.notUpdated: This item was not updated in the last three versions of the radar. If it appeared in a recent edition, it likely remains relevant. For older items, our current evaluation may differ.
labels.metaDescription: Stay at the forefront of technology trends with the Gunvor Group Technology Radar. Explore insights on languages, frameworks, methods, tools and more.
